title: Headlight Leveling System Structure Description - Control/Function - Auto Leveling System

blocks.text: Headlight Leveling System Structure Description - Control/Function - Auto Leveling System

The headlight auto-leveling control unit calculates the output voltage to the leveling motor in the ignition ON mode and when the headlights are ON, based on the input values of the current vehicle body angle, average vehicle body angle*, and vehicle speed signal, and controls the leveling motor when the control conditions are met. Note that leveling control is not applied when the vehicle speed is 1-20 km/h or 250 km/h or higher.

*Averaged vehicle body angle: The current vehicle body angle detected by the headlight leveling sensor is detected at 0.05 second intervals and averaged over 1 second, regardless of whether the headlights are on or off, after 0.1 second or more has elapsed after the ignition is turned on mode.
The leveling control output is performed when all of the following conditions are met.

items.text: More than 1.5 seconds have elapsed since the ignition ON signal was input.
Vehicle speed signal must be 0 km/h

blocks.text: The leveling control output is performed when all of the following conditions are met.

items.text: Changed by 0.2° or more in the upper/lower side relative to the current leveling control angle
The change in vehicle angle in 1 second must be within 0.087°.
More than 2 seconds have elapsed since the vehicle stopped

blocks.text: When all of the following conditions are met and continued for 25 seconds, the leveling control output is performed.

items.text: Vehicle angle change of 0.3° or less
Change in vehicle speed is less than 3 km/h
Changed by 0.2° or more in the upper/lower side relative to the current leveling control angle
